hero.alt: Giant anteater walking across green grass showing its long snout and bushy tail
hero.caption: The giant anteater has a long tube-shaped snout, a shaggy coat, and a huge bushy tail.

habitat.fileTitle: File:Tamanduá-bandeira no Parque Nacional da Serra da Canastra.jpg
habitat.alt: Giant anteater in tall golden grass on an open grassland
habitat.caption: Giant anteaters roam grasslands and savannas, sweeping the ground for ant and termite nests.
habitat.location: Serra da Canastra National Park, Brazil

diet.alt: Close-up of a giant anteater pressing its long snout to the ground to feed
diet.caption: An anteater pokes its narrow snout into nests and laps up ants and termites with a sticky tongue.

baby.alt: Baby giant anteater riding on its mother's back in grassland
baby.caption: A baby anteater, called a pup, rides on its mother's back for many months after it is born.

family.alt: Mother giant anteater carrying her pup on her back across open grass
family.caption: Anteaters live alone, so the only family group is a mother and the single pup she carries.

range.alt: Giant anteater foraging across a green Pantanal grassland
range.caption: Giant anteaters live across Central and South America, from Honduras down to northern Argentina.

size.alt: Full body of a giant anteater walking through a forest clearing
size.caption: From nose to tail, a giant anteater can stretch about seven feet long, as long as a tall person.

closeup.fileTitle: File:Brazil Mato Grosso Do Sul Pantanal Giant Antéater Grand Fourmilier Tamanoir (11851618593).jpg
closeup.alt: Close-up of a giant anteater's head and long tube-shaped snout
closeup.caption: The anteater's tiny mouth sits at the tip of its long snout and has no teeth at all.

fun-fact.alt: Giant anteater swimming across a river with its snout above the water
fun-fact.caption: Giant anteaters are strong swimmers and use their long snout like a snorkel to cross rivers.

core-habitat.alt: Giant anteater moving through tall dry grass on the savanna
core-habitat.caption: Tall grasslands and savannas give anteaters plenty of ant and termite mounds to raid.

core-diet.alt: Giant anteater with its snout lowered to the ground while foraging on a trail
core-diet.caption: An anteater can eat tens of thousands of ants and termites in a single day.

core-baby.fileTitle: File:Tamanduá bandeira com filhote nas costas - Myrmecophaga tridactyla 01.jpg
core-baby.alt: Giant anteater pup clinging to its mother's back in dry grass
core-baby.caption: The pup's stripe lines up with its mother's, helping it stay hidden while it rides along.

core-family.fileTitle: File:Tamanduá bandeira com filhote nas costas - Myrmecophaga tridactyla 02.jpg
core-family.alt: Mother giant anteater walking while carrying her pup on her back
core-family.caption: A mother carries and feeds her pup until it is big enough to find food on its own.

core-range.alt: Giant anteater crossing open grassland in the Pantanal
core-range.caption: Wetlands like the Pantanal and dry Cerrado grasslands are strongholds for giant anteaters.
